page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg8i.16xlarge is a high-frequency compute instance powered by the Intel Xeon 6462C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 64 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, resulting in 64 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It operates on the KVM hypervisor, ensuring robust virtualization and resource isolation.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage, requiring the attachment of external block storage for persistent data. Network connectivity is a key feature of this configuration, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 64 Gbps to support high-throughput data transfers. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated physical core allocation and high clock speed provide predictable performance without the resource contention typical of hyperthreaded environments, making it a solid investment for compute-heavy workloads that require consistent execution times.

Given the combination of 64 dedicated physical cores, 3.3 GHz clock speed, and 64 Gbps network bandwidth, the ecs.hfg8i.16xlarge is well-suited for compute-intensive applications, high-performance computing (HPC), large-scale web serving, and distributed compilation. The absence of local storage and GPUs makes it less ideal for deep learning training or storage-heavy database clusters, but highly effective for parallel processing, network-bound microservices, and application servers.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg8i.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6462C processor running at 3.3 GHz. With a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, it delivers 64 vCPUs without hyperthreading, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core). The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 64 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. This configuration is optimized for compute-heavy, predictable workloads such as high-performance computing, parallel processing, and network-intensive application servers.
